headline_drivers: Blowout Q1 2026 earnings beat and raise: revenue $200.3M (+63.5% YoY vs. ~$189.6M consensus), record 38.2% GAAP gross margin, backlog up to $2.2B (+20.2% QoQ), EPS loss narrowed to -$0.07 vs. -$0.12 YoY; Q2 guide raised to $225–240M revenue with 38–40% non‑GAAP gross margin (company results 2026-05-07)

Major U.S. defense wins tied to Trump’s ‘Golden Dome for America’ initiative: selection for the Space-Based Interceptor program in partnership with Raytheon plus a separate ~$190M Department of War contract, materially expanding Rocket Lab’s long‑dated defense launch and satellite pipeline (disclosed with Q1 results, early May 2026)

Acceleration in hypersonic test & launch demand: $30M Anduril Industries contract for three HASTE hypersonic test launches from LC‑2 Virginia within ~12 months, adding a visible new growth leg in defense/hypersonics (Q1 2026 disclosures)

Strategic acquisitions deepen vertically integrated space-systems capability: closing of Mynaric AG and announced deal to acquire Motiv Space Systems (Mars‑proven robotics, solar array drive assemblies), reducing key external dependencies and supporting margin expansion (Q1 2026)

Wave of bullish analyst re-ratings with sharply higher price targets after Q1: Needham to $120 (Buy), Bank of America $120, Stifel to $105 (Buy), Cantor to $96 (Overweight), Morgan Stanley to $105 (Overweight, Jan 2026), and Stephen ‘Sarge’ Guilfoyle setting a $112 target (2026-05-08–05-11), helping drive a 34% gap-up day and follow‑through buying

Technical breakout and momentum chase: stock has more than quadrupled over the past year, broke out above its prior $20.89–$105 52‑week range to new all‑time highs around $117–124 with volume ~2x 10‑day average and RSI in the 99th percentile, triggering momentum/quant screens (gapper on 2026-05-11, +17% magnitude)
summary: The recent surge in RKLB is primarily a fundamental re‑rating off a very strong Q1 2026 beat-and-raise plus a step‑function jump in long‑term visibility. Rocket Lab delivered record revenue and margins, a $2.2B backlog that’s growing faster than current sales, and high-profile U.S. defense work (Space-Based Interceptor and a separate ~$190M contract under the Golden Dome initiative, alongside a $30M Anduril hypersonics deal), all of which reposition it as a core national security and space-systems supplier rather than just a small launch company. Vertical-integration moves (Mynaric closed, Motiv acquisition) support the margin story, and a cluster of post-earnings upgrades with much higher price targets from Needham, Cantor, Stifel, Morgan Stanley, and others has validated that re‑rating in the eyes of institutions. On top of that, the stock has broken to all‑time highs on heavy volume, putting it on momentum and gapper screens, so the last 1–3 weeks’ move is a mix of fundamental repricing and technical/momentum chase rather than a sympathy move with the sector.
